summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package collects system reconnaissance (hostname, username via `whoami`, current working directory, OS details, environment variables) using `os`, `child_process`, and `fs`, then transmits the data over HTTPS to the hardcoded endpoint `https://ddjidd564.github.io`. The exfiltration logic is wired into both the `package.json` lifecycle (line 35 references `child_process`, `POST`, hostname, `process.env`, and the C2 host) and `scanner.js` (lines 142-161, 233-249, 332-337), which constructs a JSON body containing host identifiers and posts it to the same domain. The package's stated purpose (a wallet security checker) does not justify shipping installer host data to a third-party GitHub Pages account; the destination is not a vendor endpoint and is acting as attacker infrastructure for a system-information stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
